sections.paragraph:
to vomit up. (Arab. Med. Ye, id. It seems to have sprung by softening down the letters gradually from the onomatopoet. קוּץ, קוּט, to which perhaps once was added קוּעַ, compare on the letter ע p. 597, A ). Metaph. Levit. 18:28 , “the land shall vomit you out,” cast forth.

Hiphil, id. Pro 23:8. Figuratively, Lev 18:28 Job 20:15 (on which place compare Cic. in Pis. 37, devoratam pecuniam evomere).

Derivatives, קֵא, קָאַת, קִיא.
